pop-up description:
1789–1860
This era covers the early republic through the years just before the Civil War. It includes 15 presidents, from George Washington through James Buchanan. 12 out of 15 presidents had military experience, or 80%.

1861–1876
This era begins with Abraham Lincoln and covers the Civil War and early Reconstruction period. It includes 3 presidents: Lincoln, Andrew Johnson, and Ulysses S. Grant. 3 out of 3 presidents had military experience, or 100%.

1877–1916
This era covers the post-Reconstruction years, the Gilded Age, and the Progressive Era before U.S. entry into World War I. It includes 10 presidents. 6 out of 10 presidents had military experience, or 60.0%.

1917–1944
This era begins with U.S. entry into World War I and continues through World War II. It includes 5 presidents, from Woodrow Wilson through Franklin D. Roosevelt. 1 out of 5 presidents had military experience, or 20.0%.

1945–1990
This era begins after World War II and covers the period of U.S.–Soviet rivalry through George H. W. Bush. It includes 8 presidents. 8 out of 8 presidents had military experience, or 100%.

1991–present
This era covers the post-Cold War presidency, beginning with Bill Clinton. It includes 6 presidencies in this dataset. 1 out of 6 presidents had military experience, or 16.7%.
